name: Ball Python
summary: Ball pythons are calm African snakes with beautiful patterns. When frightened, they curl into a tight ball—and mothers coil around eggs to keep them warm.
metaTitle: Ball Python Facts | Habitat, Diet & Photos
metaDescription: Discover ball python facts with photos, savanna habitat, rodent diet, egg care, and defensive curling. Learn where ball pythons live and how snakes grow.
searchIntents: why is it called a ball python

lifespan.captivity: Up to 30 years in human care

speed: Slow mover; quick strike when hunting
funFacts: Ball pythons are also called royal pythons.
When scared, they curl into a ball with the head tucked inside.
Females coil around eggs and shiver to warm them.
Snakes smell with their tongue flicking in the air.
Ball pythons shed their skin as they grow.

faq.question: What is a ball python?
faq.answer: A ball python is a non-venomous snake from Africa known for curling into a ball when frightened.

faq.question: Where do ball pythons live?
faq.answer: Ball pythons live in grasslands, savannas, and forest edges in West and Central Africa.

faq.question: What do ball pythons eat?
faq.answer: Ball pythons eat rodents, small birds, and other small mammals. They swallow prey whole.

faq.question: Are ball pythons venomous?
faq.answer: No. Ball pythons are constrictors and do not have venom.

faq.question: Why is it called a ball python?
faq.answer: It curls its body into a tight ball when threatened, hiding its head in the center.

faq.question: Are snakes reptiles?
faq.answer: Yes. Snakes are reptiles with scaly skin and they lay eggs or give birth to live young depending on the species.

faq.question: Are ball pythons endangered?
faq.answer: Ball pythons are listed as Near Threatened because of habitat loss and collection for the pet trade.
